what_it_does: Defines a static 1600x900 HTML/CSS presentation frame with three separate rounded tinted plates labeled Primary, Support, and Reference, each showing a metric, label, and short descriptive fragments.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts.
Uses CSS custom properties for gold, bronze, charcoal, body text, secondary text, and line colors.
Centers a fixed-size .view artboard on a muted page background.
Adds a faint rotated grid plan texture in the upper right with a radial mask.
Places a centered title with eyebrow text and the headline "Read by tone, not by line."
Uses a three-column grid of separate rounded plates with gaps and subtly different near-white gradients.
Plate tones move from warm ivory to neutral stone to cool stone.
Each plate contains an uppercase label, large metric figure, small metric caption, and three triangle-bulleted fragments.
Includes a small circular V mark and a low-opacity diagonal stripe footer.
standouts: The visual hierarchy is carried by color temperature across three near-white surfaces.
The file explicitly frames the composition as "One surface · three zones" and "Read by tone, not by line."
Decorative details are restrained: faint plan grid, small mark, and footer texture.
conventions: Single self-contained HTML document with embedded CSS.
Fixed 1600x900 artboard using absolute positioning for major regions.
Class names are short and presentation-focused.
Uses CSS gradients, shadows, opacity, and masks for visual depth.
Breaks responsive page conventions by using fixed dimensions and no media queries.
concerns: The layout is fixed at 1600x900 and may not adapt to smaller screens.
The external Google Fonts dependency affects offline rendering.
Some decorative elements use very low opacity and may be barely visible depending on display conditions.
Several class names are abbreviated, which makes the styling less self-describing.
notes: A static design variation exploring three tonal content zones on one presentation surface.
questions: Is this intended only as a fixed slide/export, or should it respond to viewport size?
Are the metrics and fragment labels final content or placeholder copy?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents a three-zone composition using separate tinted plates and tonal distinction rather than hard dividing lines.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to show how content groups can be separated and prioritized through subtle surface tint, temperature, and spacing instead of explicit line dividers.
novelty: The distinct element is the deliberate use of three near-white plate temperatures, with the copy itself describing the visual role of each zone.
contribution: Contributes a tonal-zoning substrate: three adjacent content surfaces differentiated by subtle warmth, neutrality, and coolness, with metric blocks and short role fragments kept visually connected within one fixed artboard.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS slide showing three separate rounded near-white plates for Primary, Support, and Reference zones, using tonal differences, large metrics, and short fragments to express hierarchy.
intention_for_embedding: Created to explore a one-surface, three-zone design where viewers read structure through tone and temperature rather than explicit dividing lines.
